1.2.4 Progress academically within approved University frameworks

1.3 The Summer Semester functions as an integral component of UPES's Academic Recovery Pathways (ARP) and, where applicable, as an approved mode for Grade Improvement, subject to separate governing policies.

1.4 Participation in the Summer Semester is not automatic and is subject to eligibility conditions, attempt limits, academic feasibility, and course availability.

## 2. Scope

2.1 This Policy applies to:

2.1.1 All full-time Undergraduate and Postgraduate students of UPES
2.1.2 All Schools and Programmes where Summer Semester offerings are formally notified

2.2 For Programmes governed by statutory or professional regulatory bodies (including but not limited to the Bar Council of India (BCI) and Pharmacy Council of India (PCI)), the applicable regulatory norms shall prevail in case of any inconsistency with this Policy.

## 3. Eligibility

3.1 Debarred Students (Attendance < 75%)

Students debarred from the End-Term Examination due to attendance shortage (<75%) and awarded an F grade:

3.1.1 May be considered for Summer Semester or Directed Reading (DR) or Regular Repeat to clear the debarred course
3.1.2 Are not eligible for Supplementary Examination for the debarred course
3.1.3 Must clear the debarred course before registering for any course for which it is a prerequisite

Enrolment is subject to:

3.1.4 Availability of remaining recovery attempts under ARP
3.1.5 Course availability and academic feasibility
3.1.6 Institutional approval

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

3.2 The choice between Summer Semester, Regular Repeat and DR shall be exercised by the student, subject to course availability and eligibility conditions.

### 3.3 Academic Failure with Eligibility (Attendance ≥ 75%)

Students who receive an F grade despite meeting attendance eligibility may be considered for the Summer Semester:

- 3.3.1 After failing the regular End-Term attempt
- 3.3.2 After failing a Supplementary Examination
- 3.3.3 To manage prerequisite progression

Enrollment remains subject to:

- 3.3.4 Recovery attempt limits prescribed under ARP
- 3.3.5 Course availability and institutional feasibility

### 3.4 Grade Improvement (Through Summer Semester)

- 3.4.1 Students who apply for grade improvement under the UPES Grade Improvement Policy may utilise the Summer Semester as one of the approved modes for grade improvement.
- 3.4.2 All rules, conditions, eligibility criteria, attempt limits, transcript treatment, and CGPA implications related to grade improvement shall be governed exclusively by the UPES Grade Improvement Policy.
- 3.4.3 The Summer Semester does not create any additional entitlement, relaxation, or special consideration for grade improvement beyond what is permitted under the Grade Improvement Policy and remains subject to course availability and academic feasibility.

### 3.5 Advanced Learners (Acceleration Pathway)

Students may utilise the Summer Semester to:

- 3.5.1 Earn credits in advance
- 3.5.2 Complete prerequisite chains early

Such enrollment shall remain subject to:

- 3.5.3 Prerequisite enforcement
- 3.5.4 Programme structure requirements
- 3.5.5 Maximum Programme duration norms (N + 2 + 1)
- 3.5.6 Applicable academic and regulatory constraints

## 4. Assessment Rules

4.1 All Summer Semester courses shall follow fresh evaluation

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

4.2 No Internal Assessment (IA), Continuous Assessment (CA), or Mid-Course Evaluation (MCE) marks from previous attempts shall be carried forward
4.3 Students must complete all prescribed assessment components anew
4.4 Assessment structure and academic standards shall be identical to regular semester norms, including:
4.5 Continuous Assessment (as prescribed)
4.6 Mid-Course Evaluation
4.7 End-Term Examination

Moderation and result processing shall follow standard University examination regulations.

## 5. Course Load Limits

5.1

|  Parameter | Rule  |
| --- | --- |
|  Maximum courses per student | 2  |
|  Credit load | Not counted within the regular semester credit limit  |

5.2 Enrollment is subject to:

5.2.1 Prerequisite compliance
5.2.2 Applicable recovery or improvement attempt limits

## 6. Attempt Limits

6.1 For recovery purposes:

Each course permits:

6.1.1 One (1) Regular Attempt, and
6.1.2 A maximum of two (2) Recovery Attempts through approved recovery pathways, including Summer Semester

6.2 Where a student has failed a course and exhausted the maximum number of permitted recovery attempts under the Academic Recovery Pathways, Regular Course Repeat shall be the only permissible mechanism to continue attempts to clear the course and shall be mandatory in nature.
6.3 For grade improvement attempts undertaken through the Summer Semester, attempt counting and eligibility shall be governed solely by the Grade Improvement Policy and shall not override recovery attempt limits applicable to failed courses.

## 7. Fees

7.1 ₹3,000 per credit
7.2 Full fee payment is mandatory at the time of registration

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

7.3 Partial or delayed payments are not permitted

## Refunds

7.4 Refunds shall be permitted only in the following cases:

7.4.1 Course cancellation by the University due to insufficient enrolment
7.4.2 Verified administrative or payment error

7.5 No refunds shall be permitted for voluntary withdrawal by the student.

## 8. Attendance Requirement

8.1 Minimum 75% attendance in Summer Semester classes is mandatory
8.2 Failure to meet attendance requirements results in debarment from the Summer Semester End-Term Examination
8.3 Such courses may be cleared subsequently only if recovery attempts remain available, through:

8.3.1 Regular Repeat in a regular semester
8.3.2 Directed Reading (where permitted)
8.3.3 A future Summer Semester offering (if eligible and offered)

## 9. Course Offerings & Availability

9.1 Courses shall be offered based on student demand and academic feasibility
9.2 The University reserves the right to cancel courses that do not meet viability criteria
9.3 A student may enrol in a maximum of two courses per Summer Semester cycle

## 10. SRIJAN / Mandatory Internship Adjustment

10.1 Students enrolled in the Summer Semester may:

10.1.1 Defer SRIJAN / mandatory internship to the next available cycle without penalty, subject to University guidelines

## 11. Implications of Opting Out of the Summer Semester

11.1 Students opting not to enrol in the Summer Semester and instead repeating courses in a regular semester should note that:

11.1.1 Timetable conflicts with subsequent batches may arise
11.1.2 Examination schedules may overlap
11.1.3 Prerequisite restrictions may delay registration in advanced courses
11.1.4 Graduation and placement timelines may be impacted

11.2 While no additional fee is charged for repeating a course in a regular semester, progression delays may have academic and financial consequences.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 12. Governance & Accountability

The University reserves the right to revise fees, eligibility conditions, examination rules, timelines, and operational modalities related to the Summer Semester in line with regulatory requirements, academic considerations, or administrative exigencies. All such revisions shall be notified through official University communication channels and shall be binding.

|  Stakeholder | Responsibility  |
| --- | --- |
|  Controller of Examinations | Scheduling, conduct, and result processing  |
|  Schools / Programme Offices | Student communication and academic coordination  |
|  Students | Compliance, timely registration, and adherence to rules  |

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
0067.summary: Eligible UPES students can clear failed courses through an intensive Summer Semester as part of Academic Recovery Pathways, subject to eligibility and attempt limits.

0068.title: REGULAR COURSE REPEAT POLICY
0068.text:
# REGULAR COURSE REPEAT POLICY

(Policy Code: AG-09)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect.

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 1. Purpose

1.1 The Regular Course Repeat Policy establishes a structured academic mechanism that enables a student to re-enrol in and repeat a course through the regular semester teaching-learning process in order to:

1.1.1 Clear a failed course, or
1.1.2 Improve a previously earned grade, where permitted

1.2 This Policy:

1.2.1 Functions as a course-level Academic Recovery Pathway within the University's academic governance framework
1.2.2 Preserves full academic rigour through mandatory re-engagement with all instructional, attendance, and assessment components
1.2.3 Serves as the baseline recovery option, without facilitation, relaxation, or special accommodation
1.2.4 Operates in alignment with the UPES Academic Progression Framework, Academic Recovery Pathways (ARP)

1.3 Regular Course Repeat is designed to ensure academic integrity, progression discipline, and compliance with maximum Programme duration norms.

## 2. Scope

2.1 This Policy applies to:

2.1.1 All full-time Undergraduate and Postgraduate students across Schools and Programmes at UPES
2.1.2 All credit-bearing courses offered through the regular semester mode

2.2 For Programmes governed by statutory or professional regulatory bodies (including but not limited to the Bar Council of India (BCI) and Pharmacy Council of India (PCI)), the applicable regulatory norms shall prevail in the event of any inconsistency with this Policy.

## 3. Positioning within Academic Recovery Pathways

3.1 Regular Course Repeat is categorised as:

3.1.1 A course-level academic recovery mechanism, and
3.1.2 A permitted mechanism for grade improvement, subject to eligibility conditions, attempt limits, and progression rules

3.2 Regular Course Repeat represents the baseline academic recovery option, requiring full compliance with all regular academic rules, processes, and expectations.

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

3.3 It is distinct from **facilitated recovery pathways**, such as:
3.3.1 Supplementary Examination (assessment-only recovery)
3.3.2 Summer Semester (condensed instructional recovery)
3.3.3 Directed Reading (faculty-supervised recovery outside the regular class structure)

3.4 Facilitated recovery pathways may be advised where feasible to support progression efficiency and academic load management. However, **Regular Course Repeat remains an equally valid and academically rigorous recovery pathway**, governed strictly by this Policy.

## 4. Eligibility for Regular Course Repeat

A student may opt for or be required to repeat a course through Regular Course Repeat under one or more of the following circumstances:

4.1 To clear a **failed course** through full regular semester delivery
4.2 To **improve a previously earned grade**, where permitted
4.3 When Supplementary Examination, Summer Semester, or Directed Reading is:
4.3.1 Not available, or
4.3.2 Not opted for by the student, or
4.3.3 Not permitted under applicable rules

4.4 Where academic progression is blocked due to **prerequisite dependencies**
4.5 As part of an approved **Academic Year Back** or structured recovery decision
4.6 Where the student has **exhausted the maximum number of permitted recovery attempts** under the Academic Recovery Pathways

Eligibility is subject to:
4.7 Availability of the course in the regular semester
4.8 Compliance with semester credit limits, registration rules, and Programme duration norms

## 5. Nature of Regular Course Repeat

When a student opts for or is assigned Regular Course Repeat:

5.1 The student shall **re-register for the course as a regular student**
5.2 All components of assessment, **Continuous Assessment, Mid-Course Evaluation, and End-Term Examination**, shall be undertaken **afresh**
5.3 No internal assessment marks, attendance, or examination scores from previous attempts shall be carried forward
5.4 The student shall comply fully with:
5.4.1 Attendance requirements
5.4.2 Assessment timelines

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

5.4.3 Academic conduct and integrity norms applicable to the course

5.5 Regular Course Repeat restores the student to the standard academic conditions of the course, without exception.

## 6. Operational Considerations

6.1 As Regular Course Repeat requires full participation in the regular semester:

6.1.1 Students may experience timetable clashes with other registered courses
6.1.2 Academic workload may increase, subject to the maximum semester credit limit of 27 credits (For BCI/PCI-regulated programmes, where the prescribed semester load exceeds 24 credits, an additional credit overload of up to 15% of the semester's credit requirement may be permitted.)
6.1.3 Progression may be delayed due to prerequisite sequencing or curriculum structure

6.2 The University does not guarantee clash-free timetabling for courses repeated through the regular semester.
6.3 Students are therefore advised to carefully evaluate feasibility and to prioritise facilitated recovery pathways where available and eligible.
6.4 The student shall complete registration for the Regular Course Repeat within one (1) week from the commencement of the semester.

## 7. Credit, Grade & Transcript Treatment

7.1 Credits for the course shall be awarded only upon successful completion of the Regular Course Repeat
7.2 All failed, debarred, or ineligible attempts shall be retained in internal academic records for traceability and audit
7.3 Grade replacement and CGPA treatment shall be governed by the Academic Records & Transcript Philosophy
7.4 Transcript representation shall be neutral and non-stigmatic, reflecting final academic outcomes in accordance with University policy

## 8. Fees & Registration

8.1 Regular Course Repeat shall not attract any special recovery fee
8.2 Applicable tuition and semester fees shall follow the standard fee structure for regular enrolment
8.3 Students must complete formal registration for the repeated course within notified timelines
8.4 Failure to register appropriately may result in loss of the recovery opportunity for that semester.

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 9. Attempt Applicability & Recovery Alignment

Regular Course Repeat is a mandatory academic reset mechanism undertaken after exhaustion of permitted recovery attempts under the Academic Recovery Pathways (ARP).

### 9.1 Accordingly:

Each course permits:

- 9.1.1 One (1) Regular Attempt, and
- 9.1.2 A maximum of two (2) Recovery Attempts through approved recovery pathways
- 9.1.3 Upon exhaustion of recovery attempts, the student shall be mandatorily required to repeat the course in regular mode
- 9.1.4 All prior attempts remain part of the academic record and are considered for progression decisions within the maximum Programme duration (N + 2 + 1) prescribed by the University

## 10. Course Repeat for Grade Improvement

- 10.1 Students who are eligible for grade improvement may repeat a course in regular mode only in accordance with the UPES Grade Improvement Policy.
- 10.2 All rules, eligibility conditions, attempt limits, fee requirements, transcript treatment, and CGPA implications related to grade improvement shall be governed exclusively by the Grade Improvement Policy.
- 10.3 Nothing in this Policy shall be construed as creating an additional, independent, or automatic entitlement for grade improvement through Regular Course Repeat.

## 11. Authority & Governance

Approval, oversight, and implementation of Regular Course Repeat shall rest with the designated academic authorities as notified by the University.

- 11.1 All decisions shall be:
- 11.1.1 Rule-based
- 11.1.2 Documented
- 11.1.3 Communicated formally to the student
- 11.2 This Policy shall be applied uniformly across all Schools and Programmes.

## 12. Review & Amendment

- 12.1 This Policy shall be reviewed periodically to ensure alignment with:
- 12.1.1 UGC regulations
- 12.1.2 NEP 2020 provisions
- 12.1.3 Institutional academic strategy and progression philosophy

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

12.2 The University may revise the rules, conditions, or fees associated with this Policy, subject to applicable regulatory provisions. Such revisions shall be communicated through official University notifications and shall normally apply prospectively.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
0068.summary: Students may repeat credit-bearing courses to clear failures or improve grades by fully re-engaging with all instructional, attendance, and assessment components.

0069.title: COURSE SUBSTITUTION POLICY
0069.text:
# COURSE SUBSTITUTION POLICY

(Policy Code: AG-10)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 1. Purpose

1.1 The Course Substitution Policy defines the conditions under which a student at UPES may substitute an optional / elective course with another approved course of the same category, in order to:

1.1.1 Clear an F grade in an optional / elective course
1.1.2 Improve an earlier passing grade in an optional / elective course (limited provision)
1.1.3 Support timely academic progression without compromising academic integrity, equivalence, or regulatory compliance

1.2 This Policy operates within the University's Academic Recovery Pathways and shall be read in conjunction with the Academic Progression Framework and the UPES Academic Handbook.

## 2. Scope & Applicability

### 2.1 Applicable To

2.1.1 All Undergraduate and Postgraduate students of UPES
2.1.2 Only optional / elective courses, including:

2.1.2.1 Major Electives
2.1.2.2 Exploratory Courses
2.1.2.3 School for Life (SFL) courses

### 2.2 Not Applicable To

2.3 Mandatory / Core courses, which cannot be substituted under any circumstance

## 3. Definitions

|  Term | Definition  |
| --- | --- |
|  Course Substitution | Replacement of an approved optional course with another approved course of the same category  |
|  Optional Course | Any course not designated as mandatory for degree completion  |
|  Academic Recovery Pathways (ARP) | University-approved mechanisms for clearing academic deficiencies, including Supplementary Examination, Summer Semester, Directed Reading, Regular Course Repeat, Course Substitution, and Academic Year Back  |
|  Clearing an F Grade | Removal of an F grade by earning a passing grade (C or higher)  |
|  Grade Improvement | Replacement of a previously earned passing grade with a higher grade  |

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 4. Core Principles

4.1 Mandatory / Core courses shall not be substituted and must be cleared only by repeating the same course or through other eligible recovery pathways.

4.2 Only optional courses (Major Elective, Exploratory, SfL) are eligible for substitution.

4.3 Course substitution is a one-time academic replacement and permanently replaces the original course in the student's academic record.

4.4 Once a course is substituted, the original course:

4.4.1 Cannot be repeated
4.4.2 Cannot be improved

4.5 Course substitution shall be subject to academic equivalence review, seat availability, and approval through prescribed authorities.

4.6 Course substitution shall be counted within maximum attempt limits, as follows:

4.6.1 One (1) regular attempt and up to two (2) recovery attempts are permitted per course.
4.6.2 Upon exhaustion of permitted attempts, the student shall be mandatorily required to repeat the course in Regular Repeat mode with a subsequent batch, as per applicable policies.

## 5. Eligibility for Course Substitution

A student may apply for course substitution only if all the following conditions are met:

5.1 The course to be substituted is optional / elective
5.2 The substitution is sought for:

5.2.1 Clearing an F grade, or
5.2.2 Grade improvement, subject to limits prescribed in Section 7

5.3 The proposed substitute course belongs to the same category, as below:

5.3.1 Major Elective → Major Elective
5.3.2 Exploratory → Exploratory
5.3.3 SfL → SfL

5.4 Seat availability exists in the proposed substitute course
5.5 Academic equivalence is established and formally approved

## 6. Course Substitution for Clearing an F Grade

### 6.1 Substitution after Academic Failure

If a student receives an F grade in an optional course and chooses not to repeat the same course, the student may opt for course substitution.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

In such cases:

6.1.1 The substituted course must be of the same category
6.1.2 The grade earned in the substituted course replaces the F grade
6.1.3 The substituted course:
6.1.3.1 Counts toward credit requirements
6.1.3.2 Is included in CGPA calculation

6.2 Number of Substitutions for F-Grade Clearance

6.3 There shall be no numerical cap on the number of course substitutions permitted solely for clearing F grades, provided that:
6.3.1 Each substitution complies with this Policy
6.3.2 Academic equivalence is approved
6.3.3 Programme duration limits and attempt limits are not violated

6.4 This provision aligns with the University's approved Academic Recovery Pathways and institutional approvals.

7. Course Substitution for Grade Improvement

Course substitution for grade improvement shall be governed by the following strict conditions:

7.1 Only one (1) course substitution for grade improvement is permitted during the entire duration of the Programme.

7.2 A course substituted for grade improvement:
7.2.1 Cannot be substituted again
7.2.2 Cannot be repeated or further improved through any recovery mechanism

7.3 The grade earned in the substituted course:
7.3.1 Replaces the earlier grade for CGPA calculation
7.3.2 Appears on all subsequent transcripts

7.4 The substituted course:
7.4.1 Counts toward minimum credit requirements
7.4.2 Is included within the maximum permissible semester credit load

7.5 Approval is subject to:
7.5.1 Seat availability
7.5.2 Academic equivalence

8. Relationship with Attempt Limits & Progression Rules

8.1 Course substitution does not reset, bypass, or extend the maximum number of permitted attempts per course.

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

8.2 Failed attempts in the original course remain part of the student's academic history for progression, Year Back, and exit evaluations.

## 9. Grading & Transcript Treatment

9.1 The grade earned in the substituted course:

9.1.1 Replaces the original course grade for CGPA calculation
9.1.2 Appears on all subsequent transcripts

9.2 The original course:

9.2.1 Is marked as "Substituted"
9.2.2 Does not count toward credits or CGPA

## 10. Attendance & Assessment Requirements

10.1 Students must meet full attendance (minimum 75%) and assessment requirements of the substituted course.
10.2 No carry-forward of internal assessments or examinations from the original course is permitted.
10.3 The substituted course shall be treated as a fresh academic registration.

## 11. Approval & Workflow

|  Stage | Authority  |
| --- | --- |
|  Student Application | Student  |
|  Eligibility Verification | Programme Lead  |
|  Academic Equivalence Review | Cluster Head  |
|  Final Approval | Dean of School  |
|  Registration & Record Update | APO / CoE  |

Approval is subject to academic equivalence, seat availability, and compliance with Programme structure.

## 12. Fees

12.1 Course substitution shall not attract any additional or separate fee.
12.2 The substituted course shall be treated as a regular course registration, and the applicable charges, if any, shall be included within the regular semester fee structure, as notified by the University.
12.3 No standalone or special substitution fee shall be levied.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 13. Policy Governance & Review

13.1 This Policy shall be reviewed periodically to ensure alignment with:

- 13.1.1 UPES Academic Handbook
- 13.1.2 NEP 2020 principles
- 13.1.3 UGC regulations

Institutional academic strategy

13.2 The University reserves the right to revise eligibility conditions, procedural requirements, or applicable fees, with due notification.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
0069.summary: Students may substitute optional/elective courses (Major Electives, Exploratories, SFL) to clear F grades or improve passing grades, but cannot substitute mandatory courses.

0070.title: GRADE IMPROVEMENT POLICY
0070.text:
# GRADE IMPROVEMENT POLICY

(Policy Code: AG-11)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 1. Purpose

1.1 The Grade Improvement Policy defines the conditions under which a student at UPES may seek to improve a previously earned passing grade in an eligible course, without compromising academic rigour, progression discipline, or institutional standards.

1.2 This policy aims to:

1.2.1 Provide a limited and regulated opportunity for grade improvement
1.2.2 Prevent repeated or strategic grade enhancement attempts
1.2.3 Ensure transparency in grading, transcript treatment, and fees
1.2.4 Operate in alignment with the Academic Progression Framework and UGC norms

## 2. Scope

2.1 This policy applies to:

2.1.1 All undergraduate and postgraduate students at UPES
2.1.2 Only courses in which the student has already passed

## 3. Definitions

|  Term | Definition  |
| --- | --- |
|  Grade Improvement | A voluntary attempt to replace a previously earned passing grade with a higher grade  |
|  Improvement Attempt | A single permitted opportunity to improve a grade through an approved mode  |

## 4. Core Principles

4.1 Grade improvement is a privilege, not an entitlement.
4.2 Grade improvement is voluntary and distinct from academic recovery.
4.3 Only one improvement attempt per course is permitted.
4.4 The latest valid grade shall be used for CGPA calculation, as per University rules.

## 5 Eligibility for Grade Improvement

5.1 A student may apply for grade improvement only if:

5.1.1 The student has passed the course (grade C or higher)
5.1.2 The course is eligible for improvement as per University rules
5.1.3 The student has not already availed a grade improvement attempt for that course
5.1.4 The student is within the UGC-prescribed maximum Programme duration (N+2+1)

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 6. Modes of Grade Improvement

### 6.1 Supplementary Improvement Examination

6.1.1 The student appears only for the End-Term component in Supplementary Examination
6.1.2 Continuous Assessment marks are not re-earned
6.1.3 Applicable only for courses and cycles notified by the University
6.1.4 The improvement attempt shall be governed by all applicable rules, conditions, and limitations prescribed for Supplementary Examinations

### 6.2 Summer Semester

6.2.1 The student enrolls in the course during the Summer Semester
6.2.2 All academic requirements (attendance, assessments, examinations) shall be completed afresh
6.2.3 Grade improvement through Summer Semester shall be governed by the Summer Semester Policy
6.2.4 Enrolment is subject to:
6.2.4.1 Course offering
6.2.4.2 Seat availability
6.2.4.3 Prescribed fees

### 6.3 Course Repeat for Grade Improvement

6.3.1 The student repeats the course through regular semester enrolment
6.3.2 Governed operationally by the Regular Course Repeat Policy

### 6.4 Course Substitution for Grade Improvement

6.4.1 Applicable only to optional courses
6.4.2 Governed by the Course Substitution Policy
6.4.3 Only one substitution for grade improvement is permitted during the entire Programme
6.4.5 Course substitution for grade improvement shall not be permitted for core, mandatory, or prerequisite-bearing courses.

## 7. Limits & Attempt Applicability

7.1 Only one grade improvement attempt per course shall be permitted, irrespective of mode.
7.2 A grade improvement attempt shall be counted within the maximum number of attempts permitted per course under the Academic Progression Framework.
7.3 Grade improvement cannot be used to avoid:
7.3.1 Year Back provisions
7.3.2 Progression controls

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 8. Credit Load & Timetabling

8.1 A course taken for grade improvement:
8.1.1 Counts towards the maximum permissible credit load for the semester
8.2 The University does not guarantee resolution of timetable clashes. Any such clashes shall be the sole responsibility of the student.
8.3 Approval is subject to course availability and operational feasibility

## 9. Grading & Transcript Treatment

9.1 Upon successful completion:
9.1.1 The latest grade obtained shall replace the earlier grade for CGPA calculation
9.1.2 The improved grade shall appear on all subsequent transcripts

9.2 All prior attempts shall remain part of the internal academic record for traceability
9.3 Students who improve a course grade will not be eligible for inclusion in the university merit list or for medal awards
9.4 The new grade earned will be recorded on the transcript, along with a remark indicating it was a grade improvement attempt

## 10. Fees

10.1 A non-waivable grade improvement fee may be prescribed depending on the mode of improvement a student selects
10.2 Fees, once paid, are non-refundable
10.3 Fee applicability may vary by mode of grade improvement

## 11. Approval & Governance

11.1 Grade improvement requests shall be subject to:
11.1.1 Eligibility verification
11.1.2 Attempt-limit validation
11.1.3 Mode-specific approval as per linked policies

11.2 Decisions shall be:
11.2.1 Rule-based
11.2.2 Documented
11.2.3 Communicated formally to the student

## 12. Policy Governance & Revision

12.1 This policy shall be reviewed periodically to ensure alignment with:
12.1.1 UGC regulations

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

### 12.1.2 NEP 2020 principles

12.2 The University reserves the right to revise eligibility conditions, procedures, or applicable fees, with due notification and prospective application.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
0070.summary: UPES students can voluntarily improve one passing grade per eligible course, with the higher grade replacing the original for CGPA calculation.

0071.title: COURSE AUDIT POLICY
0071.text:
# COURSE AUDIT POLICY

(Policy Code: AG-12)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 1. Purpose

1.1 The Course Audit Policy enables students at UPES to undertake additional courses **purely for learning enrichment**, without earning academic credit and without any impact on SGPA, CGPA, academic progression, or degree completion.

1.2 Audit courses are intended to:

1.2.1 Encourage interdisciplinary and exploratory learning
1.2.2 Enable learning beyond Programme requirements
1.2.3 Support personal, professional, and intellectual development
1.2.4 Promote lifelong learning without academic or progression risk

1.3 Audit courses are **non-credit, non-CGPA, voluntary**, and **do not contribute** to degree requirements.

## 2. Scope

2.1 This policy applies to:

2.1.1 All undergraduate and postgraduate students enrolled at UPES
2.1.2 Courses explicitly approved for audit mode by the concerned School

2.2 Audit courses are undertaken **without credit accumulation** and **without impact on academic progression**.

## 3. Definitions

|  Term | Definition  |
| --- | --- |
|  Audit Course | A course taken for learning enrichment only, without academic credit and without CGPA impact  |
|  Auditing a Course | Registering for and attending a course in audit mode with the required approvals  |
|  AP / AF Outcome | Academic audit outcomes indicating Audit Pass (AP) or Audit Fail (AF), recorded as per University transcript rules  |

## 4. Nature of Audit Courses

4.1 Audit courses are:

4.1.1 Strictly **non-credit**
4.1.2 **Optional**
4.1.3 Not counted toward graduation requirements
4.1.4 Not included in SGPA or CGPA calculation
4.1.5 Audited courses shall be evaluated solely based on participation and completion, in accordance with evaluation **parameters prescribed by the Course Instructor**.

4.2 Audit courses **cannot** be converted into or used as:

4.2.1 Credit-bearing courses

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

4.2.2 Independent Study or Directed Reading
4.2.3 Course Substitution
4.2.4 Academic Recovery or Grade Improvement pathways

## 5. Eligibility

A student may audit a course only if:

5.1 The course is approved for audit mode by the School
5.2 Seat availability exists after completion of regular registrations
5.3 Any prescribed prerequisites are met
5.4 The student's regular academic commitments are not adversely affected
5.5 Audit enrolment is a privilege, not an entitlement, and is subject to approval.

## 6. Permissible Courses for Audit

Audit may be permitted for:

6.1 Elective courses
6.2 Exploratory or interdisciplinary courses
6.3 Skill-based or interest-driven offerings
6.4 Mandatory or core courses
6.5 Laboratories, studios, internships

## 7. Registration & Approval Authority

Audit registration is permitted only after completion of regular course registration.

Approval Hierarchy

|  Authority | Role  |
| --- | --- |
|  Course Instructor | Recommends audit permission and defines participation expectations  |
|  Programme Lead / Cluster Head | Confirms academic feasibility and workload  |
|  Dean of School | Final approval authority  |
|  Academic Planning & Operations (APO) | Registration and record maintenance  |
|  Controller of Examinations (CoE) | Transcript notation  |

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 8. Attendance & Participation

8.1 A minimum of 75% attendance is mandatory for successful completion of an audited course.

8.2 In addition to attendance, the Course Instructor may prescribe participation-based requirements, which may include (but are not limited to):

8.2.1 Active class engagement
8.2.2 In-class activities
8.2.3 Short projects, papers, presentations, or learning reflections
8.2.4 Other non-graded academic tasks deemed appropriate by the Instructor for the nature of the course

8.3 These requirements are evaluative in nature only for audit completion and do not carry grades or affect CGPA.

8.4 Failure to fulfil the prescribed attendance and participation requirements shall result in an Audit Fail (AF), which does not carry academic penalty or credit impact.

8.5 All audit completion criteria must be pre-announced to students at the start of the course

8.6 The defined criteria shall be:

8.6.1 Documented by the Course Instructor, and
8.6.2 Submitted to the Academic Planning & Operations (APO) for record and audit purposes

8.7 AP or AF outcomes shall be awarded strictly based on the pre-declared criteria

## 9. Assessment & Evaluation

9.1 Audit courses are not graded for SGPA or CGPA
9.2 No marks or academic credits are awarded
9.3 Informal assessments may be used only for learning feedback
9.4 Audit outcomes shall be recorded as:

9.4.1 AP (Audit Pass) – audit requirements satisfactorily met
9.4.2 AF (Audit Fail) – audit requirements not met

9.5 AP and AF are academic audit outcomes, not grades, and carry no credit or CGPA weightage.

9.6 Transcript Recording for Audit Courses

9.6.1 AP and AF outcomes shall appear in the Semester Transcript
9.6.2 In the Final Transcript and all consolidated transcripts issued thereafter, only audited courses with an AP (Audit Pass) outcome shall be reflected
9.6.3 Audit Fail (AF) outcomes shall not be displayed in the Final Transcript

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

9.7 Audited courses reflected in the Final Transcript shall:
- 9.7.1 Appear without academic credit,
- 9.7.2 Have no SGPA or CGPA impact, and
- 9.7.3 Be clearly identified as Audit Courses

10. Fees
- 10.1 Audit courses are ordinarily offered without fee
- 10.2 The University reserves the right to prescribe a nominal audit fee, if required, with approval of the competent authority and prior notification

11. Restrictions & Safeguards
11.1 Audit courses:
- 11.1.1 Do not satisfy degree credit requirements
- 11.1.2 Cannot be used to meet prerequisites
- 11.1.3 Cannot replace failed or required courses
- 11.1.4 Cannot be used for academic recovery, grade improvement, progression, ranking, honours, or merit determination
- 11.2 Audit enrolment shall not dilute academic standards or disrupt regular teaching-learning processes.

12. Governance & Record Management
12.1 Audit approvals and outcomes shall be:
- 12.1.1 Rule-based
- 12.1.2 Documented
- 12.1.3 Uniformly applied across Schools
- 12.2 All records shall be maintained by the Academic Planning & Operations (APO) and Controller of Examinations (CoE) for audit and transcript purposes.

13. Policy Review
13.1 This policy shall be reviewed periodically to ensure alignment with:
- 13.1.1 NEP 2020 principles
- 13.1.2 Institutional academic strategy
- 13.2 Any revisions shall be notified prospectively through official University communication.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
0071.summary: Students may audit approved courses for learning enrichment without earning credits or impacting GPA/progression, with outcomes recorded as AP or AF.

0072.title: INTERNAL ACADEMIC MOBILITY
0072.text:
# INTERNAL ACADEMIC MOBILITY

(Change of Programme / Change of Major / Change of Specialisation)

(Policy Code: AG-13)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect.

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 1. Purpose

1.1 This Policy establishes a comprehensive, regulated, and uniform framework governing internal academic mobility at UPES, limited to:

1.1.1 Change of Programme
1.1.2 Change of Major
1.1.3 Change of Specialisation

1.2 The Policy enables limited academic mobility while safeguarding:

1.2.1 Academic rigour and Programme learning outcomes
1.2.2 Prerequisite integrity and curriculum sequencing
1.2.3 Fairness, transparency, and auditability
1.2.4 Student protection against academic and regulatory risk

1.3 This Policy operates strictly within UGC-prescribed maximum Programme duration norms (N + 2 + 1) and aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, without diluting institutional standards.

## 2. Scope & Applicability

This Policy applies to:

2.1 All full-time Undergraduate and Postgraduate students of UPES
2.2 All Schools, Programmes, Majors, and Specialisations offered by the University

## 3. Core Principles

All internal academic mobility decisions shall be governed by the following principles:

3.1 Academic mobility is a privilege, not an entitlement
3.2 No automatic right to credit transfer or credit carry-forward exists
3.3 Academic feasibility shall override student preference
3.4 Prerequisite and curriculum integrity shall not be compromised
3.5 Seat availability and faculty capacity are mandatory conditions
3.6 All changes must remain within the maximum Programme duration (N + 2 + 1)
3.7 All approvals must be formally documented and ERP-recorded

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 4. Definitions

|  Term | Definition  |
| --- | --- |
|  Academic Mobility | A formally approved change within a student’s enrolled academic structure, governed by this Policy.  |
|  Change of Programme | A change from one degree Programme to another, resulting in a change in the degree pursued.  |
|  Change of Major | A change in the primary disciplinary focus within the same degree Programme.  |
|  Change of Specialisation | A change within approved specialisation tracks of a major, without altering the degree or major.  |
|  Credit Mapping | An academic equivalence exercise to determine whether previously earned credits align with course learning outcomes in the revised academic structure.  |
|  Academic Equivalence | A determination by faculty that two courses are comparable in level, depth, content, and intended learning outcomes.  |
|  Excess/Surplus Credits | Credits earned by a student that are not mapped to graduation requirements under the revised academic structure.  |

## 5. Common Eligibility Conditions

5.1 Across all pathways covered under this Policy, a student must:

5.1.1 Be formally enrolled at UPES
5.1.2 Have no pending disciplinary proceedings
5.1.3 Meet minimum academic standing requirements as prescribed by the School
5.1.4 Satisfy academic feasibility, capacity, and regulatory constraints
5.1.5 Meeting eligibility conditions does not guarantee approval.

### 5.2 Application Window

Applications for Change of Programme, Change of Major, or Change of Specialisation shall be accepted only within the following timelines:

5.2.1 First Semester of the Programme

Applications must be submitted no later than the commencement of mid-term examinations of the first semester.

5.2.2 After Completion of First Semester

Applications must be submitted within the first week of the commencement of the relevant semester.

Applications submitted after the first week of the semester shall not be entertained.

5.2.3 Late or Out-of-Window Applications

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

No application shall be considered if the proposed change places the student at risk of exceeding the maximum Programme duration (N + 2 + 1).

Requests submitted beyond the prescribed timelines shall be **summarily rejected**, without appeal on grounds of preference or hardship.

## 6. Credit Mapping & Academic Equivalence

6.1 Credit mapping is an **academic equivalence exercise**, not an entitlement
6.2 No automatic or blanket credit transfer shall be permitted
6.3 Mapping decisions must be:

6.3.1 Academically justified,
6.3.1 Documented in the prescribed Credit Mapping & Equivalence Form (Annexure 2), and
6.3.1 Auditable

6.4 Unmapped credits may be classified as:

6.4.1 Exploratory credits wherever feasible, or
6.4.1 Excess/surplus credits not contributing to graduation

6.5 Credit mapping shall not:

6.5.1 Dilute Programme learning outcomes, or
6.5.2 Bypass mandatory core courses or prerequisite chains

6.6 All mapping decisions require School-level approval and ERP recording.

## 7. Limits & Restrictions

7.1 Internal academic mobility under this Policy shall ordinarily be permitted **only once** during a Programme
7.2 Late-stage requests may be restricted or denied due to curriculum feasibility
7.3 No change shall be permitted if it results in:

7.3.1 Extension beyond maximum Programme duration (N+2+1), or
7.3.2 Compromise of academic standards or regulatory compliance

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 8. Governance & Approval Structure

|  Authority | Responsibility  |
| --- | --- |
|  Programme Lead / Cluster Head | Academic feasibility and credit mapping review  |
|  Dean of School | Academic approval  |
|  Registrar / Vice Provost | Regulatory and duration compliance  |
|  Academic Planning & Operations (APO) | ERP implementation  |

No academic change shall be valid unless reflected in the ERP system.

## 9. Appeals

9.1 Appeals may be submitted only on procedural grounds
9.2 Appeals shall not be entertained on grounds of preference, expectation, or academic judgement
9.3 Appeal authority and timelines shall be notified separately

## 10. Change of Programme

### 10.1 Nature and Definition

Change of Programme refers to a degree-level transition wherein a student shifts from one approved degree Programme to another, resulting in a change in the qualification pursued (e.g., BBA to BTech, BA to BSc, MBA to MTech).

### 10.2 Eligibility Conditions

A student may be considered for Change of Programme only if:

10.2.1 The student satisfies the minimum admission eligibility criteria of the target Programme, including subject background and applicable regulatory requirements
10.2.2 The student applies within the permissible semester window notified by the University
10.2.3 The student is in acceptable academic standing, as determined by the School

10.2.4 Seat availability exists in the target Programme

Meeting the above eligibility conditions does not guarantee approval.

### 10.3 Programme Duration Reset

10.3.1 Upon approval of Change of Programme, the student shall be treated as a fresh entrant to the new Programme for the purpose of maximum Programme duration.

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

10.3.2 Accordingly, the N + 2 + 1 time limit shall apply independently to the new Programme, and time spent in the previous Programme shall not be counted toward this limit, subject to approved credit transfer and equivalence norms.

### 10.4 Credit Transfer and Semester Placement

10.4.1 Notwithstanding Clause 10.3, credits earned by the student prior to the Change of Programme shall remain preserved in the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC).

10.4.2 The University may, subject to approved academic equivalence, prerequisite compliance, regulatory requirements, and availability of vacancies, permit transfer or mapping of eligible credits and place the student in an appropriate semester (including beyond Semester 1).

10.4.3 Such semester placement shall not alter the reset of the maximum Programme duration, and the N + 2 + 1 limit shall apply independently to the new Programme, irrespective of credit transfer.

### 10.5 Credit Mapping and Academic Treatment

10.5.1 There shall be no automatic transfer of credits between Programmes

10.5.2 Credits shall be mapped only where academic equivalence is established

10.5.3 Credits that cannot be mapped shall be classified as:

- Exploratory credits, or
- Excess / surplus credits not contributing to graduation

10.5.4 The student shall acknowledge that significant credit loss may occur as a result of Change of Programme

10.5.5 Credits earned prior to the Change of Programme shall remain preserved in the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) for a maximum validity period of seven (7) years, and may be transferred or mapped only where academic equivalence, prerequisite compliance, and regulatory requirements are fulfilled, in accordance with University and UGC norms

10.5.6 In accordance with UGC regulations, only credits earned in Academic Year 2021-22 and thereafter shall be eligible for credit accumulation, transfer, and redemption through the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC)

### 10.6 Timing and Stage Restrictions

10.6.1 Change of Programme shall ordinarily be permitted only in early semesters, as notified by the University

10.6.2 Requests at advanced stages may be rejected due to:

- Curriculum sequencing constraints

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

- Prerequisite misalignment
- Graduation feasibility risk

### 10.7 Additional Safeguards

10.7.1 For undergraduate Programmes, a Parent / Guardian Undertaking shall be mandatory

10.7.2 A revised academic plan and graduation timeline shall apply
10.7.3 The student shall be governed by the prevailing curriculum of the target Programme

## 11. Change of Major

### 11.1 Nature and Definition

Change of Major refers to a disciplinary shift within the same degree Programme, wherein a student changes the primary field of study while continuing within the same degree framework (e.g., BTech Mechanical to BTech Computer Science, BBA Marketing to BBA Finance).

Change of Major does not alter the degree awarded but significantly impacts curriculum depth, prerequisites, and sequencing.

### 11.2 Eligibility Conditions

A student may be considered for Change of Major only if:

11.2.1 The change occurs within the same degree Programme
11.2.2 The student satisfies academic preparedness requirements for the target major
11.2.3 All prerequisite conditions prescribed by the target major can be met
11.2.4 Seat availability exists in the target major
11.2.5 The change does not compromise graduation within maximum duration (N+2+1)

### 11.3 Credit Mapping and Curriculum Alignment

11.3.1 Credits already earned shall be mapped only where academic equivalence exists
11.3.2 Core courses of the new major that were not previously undertaken must be completed
11.3.3 Credit mapping shall not:

11.3.3.1 Bypass prerequisite chains, or
11.3.3.2 Dilute discipline depth

Unmapped credits may be retained as exploratory or excess/surplus credits.

### 11.4 Timing and Limits

11.4.1 Change of Major shall ordinarily be permitted only once during the Programme
11.4.2 Requests made after substantial completion of major-specific coursework may be denied

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

### 11.5 Academic Progression Post-Change

11.5.1 The student shall follow the **approved academic structure** of the new major
11.5.2 Prerequisite enforcement shall apply strictly
11.5.3 Graduation timelines may be revised based on feasibility

## 12. Change of Specialisation

### 12.1 Nature

Change of Specialisation refers to a change between approved specialisation tracks **within the same major and degree**, without altering the Programme or major.

### 12.2 Eligibility & Conditions

Change of Specialisation may be permitted subject to:

12.2.1 Availability of the target specialisation,
12.2.2 Compliance with prescribed prerequisites, and
12.2.3 Academic feasibility within the approved Programme duration.

### 12.3 Credit Treatment

12.3.1 Common core and major credits shall be retained.
12.3.2 Specialisation-specific credits shall be mapped only where academically equivalent.
12.3.3 Unmapped credits, if any, shall not contribute to specialisation completion.

### 12.4 Progression & Completion

The student shall be governed by the **requirements of the target specialisation**, and all prescribed specialisation credits must be completed for transcript recognition.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
0072.summary: This policy governs internal academic mobility (programme/major/specialisation changes) for full-time UPES students as a privilege, with no automatic credit transfer within UGC duration limits.

0073.title: POLICY ON CHANGE OF MINOR
0073.text:
# POLICY ON CHANGE OF MINOR

(Policy Code: AG-14)

Applicable from Admission Year 2026

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 1. Purpose

1.1 This Policy defines the institutional framework, eligibility conditions, and governing principles for Change of Minor for undergraduate students at UPES.

1.2 The Policy aims to:

1.2.1 Enable structured academic flexibility and informed student choice in alignment with NEP 2020
1.2.2 Ensure academic coherence, prerequisite integrity, and credit feasibility across Programmes
1.2.3 Prevent misuse, dilution, or partial recognition of Minor credentials
1.2.4 Maintain consistency, transparency, and audit readiness across all Schools

1.3 Change of Minor is a regulated academic flexibility and not an entitlement.

## 2. Scope

2.1 This Policy applies to:

2.1.1 All full-time undergraduate students enrolled in three-year and four-year degree Programmes at UPES
2.1.2 All formally approved Minor Programmes offered by UPES Schools

## 3. Definitions

|  Term | Definition  |
| --- | --- |
|  Minor | A structured and approved set of courses (typically 24 credits) pursued outside the student's Major discipline, which is reflected on the final transcript and degree certificate only upon full completion.  |
|  Change of Minor | The formal academic process through which a student discontinues an approved Minor and opts for a different Minor, subject to eligibility conditions, prescribed timelines, academic feasibility, and institutional approval.  |
|  Exploratory Credits | University-approved courses that allow students to explore disciplines outside their declared Major or Minor without commitment to a credential.  |
|  Excess/Surplus Credits (Non-Contributory) | Credits earned by a student that are retained on the academic record but do not contribute toward Major, Minor, or graduation credit requirements.  |

## 4. Guiding Principles

4.1 Change of Minor at UPES is governed by the following principles:

4.1.1 Flexibility with accountability - Minor changes are permitted early in the academic journey, but not indefinitely

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

4.1.2 No academic dilution – A Minor is awarded only upon full and complete fulfilment of prescribed requirements
4.1.3 No retroactive entitlement – Credits already earned do not guarantee automatic mapping to a new Minor
4.1.4 Prerequisite integrity – Foundational sequencing and learning progression must be preserved
4.1.5 Time-bound decision-making – Late-stage changes are restricted to safeguard academic coherence
4.1.6 Capacity-linked approval – Approval is subject to seat availability, faculty capacity, timetable feasibility, and academic resources

4.2 The University does not guarantee approval of Change of Minor solely on the basis of student eligibility.

## 5. Eligibility Conditions

5.1 A student may apply for Change of Minor only if all the following conditions are met:

|  Condition | Requirement  |
| --- | --- |
|  Application Window | Application submitted no later than the end of Semester 3  |
|  Credit Feasibility | Completion of the proposed Minor is feasible within the maximum permissible Programme duration  |
|  Prerequisites | Entry prerequisites for the proposed Minor are fully satisfied  |
|  Load Compliance | Per-semester and total Programme credit limits are not breached  |
|  Seat Availability | Seats are available in the proposed Minor, as determined by the offering School  |

5.2 The prescribed application window ensures sufficient remaining semesters to complete the Minor without compromising Programme duration norms.

## 6. Permissible Scenarios

### 6.1 Early-Stage Change (Preferred)

Where the student has completed Exploratory credits or not more than 6 credits of the originally declared Minor:

Completed courses may be:

6.1.1 Reclassified as Exploratory credits, or
6.1.2 Mapped to the new Minor only if explicitly approved
6.1.3 Treated as excess / surplus, non-contributory credits where such courses are not mapped to any academic category.
6.1.4 No assurance of credit carry-forward is provided

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

### 6.2 Mid-Stage Change (Restricted)

Where the student has completed more than 6 but fewer than 12 credits of the originally declared Minor:

Change of Minor may be permitted only if:

6.2.1 Academic feasibility is clearly demonstrated, and
6.2.2 The student acknowledges that completion of the new Minor may require enrolment in Summer Semester and/or Directed Reading (DR) to remain within the maximum permissible Programme duration (N+2+1)

No extension beyond the maximum permissible Programme duration shall be granted on account of Change of Minor.

### 6.3 Late-Stage Change (Not Permitted)

Where the student has completed 12 or more credits of a declared Minor:

6.3.1 Change of Minor shall not be permitted
6.3.2 The student must either:

6.3.2.1 Complete the declared Minor, or
6.3.2.2 Continue remaining courses, if any, as Exploratory credits if feasible (no Minor credential)

## 7. Credit Treatment Rules

|  Situation | Credit Treatment  |
| --- | --- |
|  Courses common to both Minors | May be mapped to the new Minor, subject to academic equivalence review and formal approval.  |
|  Non-overlapping courses | Shall be reclassified as excess / surplus credits or Exploratory credits, as determined by the academic approving authority.  |
|  Partial completion of a Minor | No Minor credential shall be awarded unless all prescribed Minor requirements are fully completed.  |
|  Surplus credits | Shall not reduce Major, Minor, or overall graduation credit requirements.  |
|  Transcript treatment of surplus credits | Surplus credits shall remain visible on the academic record but shall not contribute toward degree or credential fulfilment.  |

## 8. Impact on Degree & Transcript

8.1 Only fully completed Minors shall appear on the final transcript and degree certificate
8.2 Discontinued or partially completed Minors shall not be mentioned
8.3 Exploratory and surplus credits shall continue to appear in semester-wise transcripts

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 9. Approval & Process Overview

9.1 All Change of Minor requests shall undergo a structured academic review, including:

9.1.1 Verification of eligibility conditions
9.1.2 Academic feasibility assessment and credit mapping
9.1.3 Confirmation of seat availability by the offering School
9.1.4 Determination of whether Summer Semester and/or Directed Reading (DR) is required for timely completion

9.2 Final approval shall rest with the designated academic authority, as notified by the University.
9.3 Detailed operational steps, documentation requirements, and timelines shall be governed through a separate Standard Operating Procedure (Annexure 1).

## 10. Limitations & Restrictions

10.1 A student may change Minor only once during the entire Programme
10.2 Change of Minor cannot be combined with:

10.2.1 Change of Programme or Change of Major, or
10.2.2 Special credit overloads, relaxations, or exemptions

10.3 Where multiple academic change requests exist, Change of Programme or Major shall take precedence
10.4 Summer Semester and Directed Reading (DR), if prescribed for Minor completion, shall attract applicable fees, as notified by the University
10.5 No fee waivers or exceptions shall apply unless expressly approved by the competent authority

## 11. Interaction with Other Academic Changes

11.1 Change of Major may require reassessment or discontinuation of an approved Minor
11.2 Change of Programme shall automatically supersede any approved Minor unless explicitly retained by the University

## 12. Exceptional Cases

12.1 In rare and well-justified circumstances—such as Minor discontinuation, Programme restructuring, or regulatory change—the University may permit deviation from this Policy subject to approval by the competent authority.
12.2 Such approvals shall be:

12.2.1 Exception-based, and
12.2.2 Non-precedent setting

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 13. Policy Governance, Review & Effective Date

13.1 Right to Revise Minor Structure: The University and the concerned School reserve the right to revise, modify, restructure, suspend, or withdraw Minor offerings, including credit structure and course composition, in accordance with academic requirements, regulatory directions, and institutional priorities.

13.2 Effective Date: This Policy shall be effective from Academic Year 2026-27 and shall apply to all students seeking Change of Minor from this academic year onwards.

13.3 Periodic Review: This Policy shall be reviewed periodically to ensure continued alignment with:

13.3.1 NEP 2020

13.3.2 Institutional academic strategy

13.4 Change of Minor is a regulated academic provision and not an entitlement. All determinations relating to eligibility, credit treatment, academic feasibility, equivalence, and approval shall be final and binding, as decided by the competent academic authorities of the University.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
0073.summary: The policy allows undergraduate students to formally change their Minor programme, subject to meeting eligibility conditions, prescribed timelines, academic feasibility requirements, and obtaining institutional approval.

0074.title: LATERAL ENTRY & INTER-INSTITUTIONAL MIGRATION ADMISSIONS POLICY
0074.text:
UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

# LATERAL ENTRY & INTER-INSTITUTIONAL MIGRATION ADMISSIONS POLICY

(Policy Code: AG-15)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect.

January 2026
0074.summary: This policy governs lateral entry and inter-institutional transfer admissions, effective January 2026 with code AG-15.

0075.title: Lateral Entry and Inter-Institutional Migration Admissions Policy Framework
0075.text:
UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

# LATERAL ENTRY & INTER-INSTITUTIONAL MIGRATION ADMISSIONS POLICY

(Policy Code: AG-15)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

# 1. Purpose

1.1 This Policy establishes a transparent, student-centric, and regulator-compliant framework governing:

1.2 This Policy defines a regulator-compliant and academically robust framework governing:

1.2.1 Multiple entry options, and
1.2.2 Lateral entry

1.3 Into Undergraduate (UG) and Postgraduate (PG) Programmes of the University, in alignment with:

1.3.1 National Education Policy (NEP) 2020
1.3.2 UGC Guidelines on Multiple Entry and Exit in Academic Programmes (July 2021)
1.3.3 Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) Regulations

1.4 The Policy ensures:

1.4.1 Parity with regular entrants,
1.4.2 Structured recognition of prior learning, and
1.4.3 Seamless academic mobility without compromise on academic standards.

# 2. Scope & Applicability

2.1 This Policy applies to:

2.1.1 All Undergraduate and Postgraduate Programmes offered by the University.
2.1.2 Admissions through:

2.1.2.1 Regular Entry, and
2.1.2.2 Lateral Entry at approved academic levels only.

2.2 Where lateral entry is not permitted due to statutory or academic constraints, the same shall be explicitly notified in the Admission Prospectus and University website for the relevant admission cycle.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

# 3. Definitions

3.1

|  Term | Definition  |
| --- | --- |
|  Entry Options | Recognised academic entry points into an ongoing degree Programme, based on earned credits and qualifications, as permitted under UGC norms.  |
|  Lateral Entry | Admission into a Programme beyond the first year / first semester, based on recognised prior credits, established academic equivalence, and availability of approved vacancies.  |
|  Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) | A national digital repository that enables accumulation, transfer, and redemption of academic credits to support multiple entry pathways under applicable regulations.  |
|  Lateral Entry (Undergraduate - UPES) | Admission into an ongoing undergraduate Programme at UPES, normally into Semester 3, based on prior learning completed at a recognised institution (such as a diploma or equivalent qualification), in accordance with applicable regulatory norms.  |
|  Inter-Institutional Migration (Migration) | Transfer of a currently enrolled undergraduate student from another recognised university into the same or an academically equivalent Programme at UPES, typically after completion of Semester 1 or Semester 2, subject to availability of vacancies, regulatory permissions, and academic equivalence approval.  |
|  Credit Equivalence | A structured academic evaluation involving course-wise mapping of Course Learning Outcomes (CLOs), content coverage, assessment rigour, credits, and contact hours to determine parity with the UPES curriculum.  |
|  Bridge Course | A structured academic intervention prescribed to address credit, content, or prerequisite gaps, delivered through Junior Batch enrolment, Directed Reading (DR), and/or Summer Semester or Special Term.  |
|  Credit Transfer & Equivalence Committee (CTEC)* | A statutory University committee responsible for reviewing, approving, and recording all credit equivalence, credit transfer, and bridge course decisions under this Policy.  |
|  Vacancy | A seat that remains unfilled after completion of the regular admission process for the relevant academic year and Programme, as approved by the competent authority.  |

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

# 3.2 Credit Transfer & Equivalence Committee (CTEC) Composition:

3.2.1 Chair: Vice Provost
3.2.2 Members:

3.2.2.1 School Nominee (Programme Expert)
3.2.2.2 Controller of Examinations
3.2.2.3 Academic Planning & Operations (APO) Representative
3.2.2.4 Student Records Representative

3.2.3 Quorum: Minimum three (3) members

3.3 Where a School Expert involved in course evaluation is also a CTEC member, the individual shall recuse themselves from the final decision, and the Chair may nominate an alternate expert to avoid conflict of interest.

# 4. Applicability by Admission Route

4.1

|  Parameter | Lateral Entry | Migration  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  Entry Semester | Odd Semester | Semester 2 onwards  |
|  Prior Academic Status | Diploma / Degree holder | Enrolled UG student  |
|  Regulatory Basis | AICTE / PCI / BCI norms | UGC Migration norms  |
|  Intake Limit | ≤10% of sanctioned intake | Vacancy-based only  |
|  Credit Mapping | Mandatory | Mandatory  |
|  Bridge Courses | Permitted | Permitted  |

4.2 Permissible UG Entry Levels

|  Entry Point | Academic Requirement | Entry Allowed In  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  Entry at Year 1 | Grade 12 / equivalent qualification | Semester 1  |
|  Entry at Year 2 | Undergraduate Certificate (36-40 credits or more, Level 5) | Semester 3  |
|  Entry at Year 3 | Undergraduate Diploma (72-80 credits or more, Level 6) | Semester 5  |

4.2 Permissible PG Entry Levels

|  PG Programme Type |   | Entry Qualification | Entry Allowed In  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  One-year Programme | Master's | Four-year Undergraduate's Degree (Honours / Research) | Semester 1  |
|  Two-year Programme | Master's | Undergraduate's Degree (3-year or 4-year) | Semester 1  |

January 2026
0075.summary: This policy establishes a framework for lateral entry admissions into UG/PG programmes aligned with NEP 2020 and UGC guidelines, ensuring parity with regular entrants.

0076.title: Credit Transfer, Equivalence, and Bridge Course Framework
0076.text:
UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

# 3.2 Credit Transfer & Equivalence Committee (CTEC) Composition:

3.2.1 Chair: Vice Provost
3.2.2 Members:

3.2.2.1 School Nominee (Programme Expert)
3.2.2.2 Controller of Examinations
3.2.2.3 Academic Planning & Operations (APO) Representative
3.2.2.4 Student Records Representative

3.2.3 Quorum: Minimum three (3) members

3.3 Where a School Expert involved in course evaluation is also a CTEC member, the individual shall recuse themselves from the final decision, and the Chair may nominate an alternate expert to avoid conflict of interest.

# 4. Applicability by Admission Route

|  Entry Semester | Odd Semester | Semester 2 onwards  |

UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

# 5. Eligibility

## 5.1 General Conditions

Admission under this Policy shall be permitted only if:

5.1.1 Entry is sought into approved semesters as notified by the University
5.1.2 The applicant has no unresolved academic backlogs or failures at the source institution at the time of final admission confirmation
5.1.3 The applicant meets the minimum eligibility criteria prescribed for the Programme
5.1.4 The applicant satisfies regulatory requirements applicable to the discipline
5.1.5 The School may conduct written tests and/or interviews as part of academic screening

5.2 A relaxation of up to 10% in eligibility may be granted to outstanding sportspersons, subject to:

5.2.1 Applicable UGC / Sports Authority of India guidelines, and
5.2.2 Approval of the Admissions Supervision Committee.

## 5.3 Programme-Specific Eligibility

Programme-wise eligibility conditions shall be specified in Annexure 1 and notified for each admission cycle.

# 6. Intake, Vacancies & Reservation

6.1 Lateral Entry admissions shall not exceed 10% of the sanctioned intake or the regulator-prescribed cap, whichever is lower
6.2 Migration admissions shall be permitted strictly against existing vacancies
6.3 No supernumerary seats shall be created unless explicitly permitted by the regulator
6.4 PCI norms (Pharmacy) and BCI Rules (Law) shall apply wherever relevant
6.5 Statutory reservation norms shall apply as per UPES policy

Change of Programme, Major, or Specialisation after admission under this Policy shall be governed only by the UPES Internal Academic Mobility Policy and within prescribed application windows.

# 7. University Discretion

7.1 Admission through Lateral Entry or Inter-Institutional Migration is not an entitlement.
7.2 Meeting minimum eligibility criteria does not guarantee admission.
7.3 The University reserves the right to approve or reject applications based on:

7.3.1 Academic equivalence outcomes,
7.3.2 Capacity constraints,
7.3.3 Regulatory requirements, and

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

7.3.4 Institutional academic priorities.

## 8. Credit Transfer & Equivalence Framework

### 8.1 Applicants shall submit:

#### 8.1.1 Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) ID and/or official transcripts

[Note: Credits earned by a student prior to admission through Lateral Entry or Inter-Institutional Migration shall remain preserved in the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) for a maximum validity period of seven (7) years, as per applicable UGC norms.

Such credits may be considered for transfer, accumulation, or mapping to the new Programme only where academic equivalence, prerequisite compliance, and applicable regulatory requirements are fully satisfied, and subject to approval by the competent academic authority of the University. Further, in accordance with UGC regulations, only credits earned in Academic Year 2021–22 and thereafter shall be eligible for credit accumulation, transfer, and redemption through the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC).

#### 8.1.2 Detailed syllabus including credits, contact hours, CLOs, and assessment schemes

### 8.2 Evaluation Parameters

Credit equivalence shall be evaluated based on:

- 8.2.1 CLO alignment
- 8.2.2 Content overlap
- 8.2.3 Assessment rigour
- 8.2.4 Credit and contact hour matching

Evaluation shall be conducted using a documented academic rubric approved by CTEC.

### 8.3 Equivalence Thresholds

- 8.3.1 ≥70% similarity: Equivalent — credit transferred
- 8.3.2 50–69% similarity: Conditional equivalence with mandatory bridge courses
- 8.3.3 <50% similarity: Not equivalent

All partial or conditional equivalence decisions shall be explicitly justified and recorded in CTEC minutes.

### 8.4 Credit Handling Rules

- 8.4.1 Where UPES credit value is higher, the deficit shall be addressed through bridge courses/modules
- 8.4.2 Where external credit value is higher, UPES credit value shall prevail
- 8.4.3 All recognised credits shall be posted to the Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) using University-approved templates

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

# 9. Prerequisite Enforcement

UPES follows a strict prerequisite-based academic progression model.

9.1 Prerequisite alignment shall be verified for all mapped courses and future registrations
9.2 Prerequisites shall not be waived under any circumstances
9.3 All gaps shall be recorded and addressed through an approved Bridge Plan
9.4 Students shall not be permitted to register for a course unless prerequisites are fulfilled

# 10. Bridge Courses

10.1 Bridge courses prescribed under this Policy shall be:

10.1.1 Designed and delivered by the respective School, and
10.1.1 Academically equivalent to regular courses.

10.2 Permitted Modes

10.2.1 Regular Course Repeat
10.2.2 Directed Reading (DR)
10.2.3 Summer Semester
10.2.4 MOOCs

10.3 A maximum of two (2) bridge courses per term may be prescribed.
10.4 Any exception beyond this limit shall require approval of the Pro Vice Chancellor.

# 10.5 Fee Applicability for Bridge Courses

Bridge courses shall be governed by the following fee rules:

10.5.1 Bridge courses undertaken during a regular semester, as part of approved semester registration, shall be governed by the applicable regular semester fee structure and shall not attract any additional or separate bridge course fee.
10.5.2 Bridge courses undertaken through Summer Semester or Directed Reading (DR) shall attract applicable fees, as notified by the University from time to time.
10.5.3 Fee liability for Summer Semester and Directed Reading bridge courses is mandatory and non-waivable, irrespective of the mode of admission.

No exemption, waiver, or adjustment of fees shall be permitted solely on the basis of admission through Lateral Entry or Inter-Institutional Migration.

# 10.6 Academic Rules for Bridge Courses

Bridge courses shall:

10.6.1 Carry regular academic credits,
10.6.2 Follow standard attendance, assessment, and grading rules, and
10.6.3 Be governed by UPES academic regulations.

January 2026
