0078.title: ACADEMIC APPEALS & RE-EVALUATION POLICY
0078.text:
# ACADEMIC APPEALS & RE-EVALUATION POLICY

(Policy Code: AG-16)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 1. PURPOSE

1.1 The UPES Academic Appeals & Re-evaluation Policy establishes a structured, transparent, confidential, and time-bound framework for the resolution of academic appeals and examination re-evaluation requests.

1.2 This Policy is intended to:

1.2.1 Uphold principles of natural justice, procedural fairness, and consistency

1.2.2 Clearly distinguish examination re-evaluation processes from non-examination academic appeals, and define appropriate authority and finality for each

1.2.3 Prevent ad-hoc, discretionary, or parallel appeal mechanisms

1.2.4 Protect academic standards while ensuring fairness to students

## 2. SCOPE & APPLICABILITY

This Policy applies to all undergraduate and postgraduate students of UPES.

It governs:

2.1 Appeals related to non-examination academic decisions

2.2 Requests and appeals related to End-Term examination re-evaluation

2.3 Appeals arising from academic mobility decisions, including Change of Programme, Change of Major, Change of Specialisation, and Change of Minor

## 3. TERMINOLOGY CLARIFICATION

3.1 For the purposes of this Policy:

3.1.1 Academic Appeal refers to a challenge raised against a non-examination academic decision, limited strictly to procedural error, administrative error, or incorrect application of published academic rules.

3.1.2 Re-evaluation refers exclusively to the review of End-Term examination answer scripts, conducted under the authority of the Controller of Examinations.

3.2 No academic appeal shall be treated as a re-evaluation request, and no re-evaluation request shall be treated as an academic appeal.

## 4. MATTERS NOT COVERED UNDER THIS POLICY

The following matters are expressly excluded and shall not be appealable under this Policy:

4.1 Academic judgment regarding the quality of answers, merit of performance, or grading discretion

4.2 Course design, syllabus content, curriculum structure, assessment weightages, or pedagogy approved by statutory bodies

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

4.3 Attendance condonation decisions or policy-based attendance determinations
4.4 Consequences of non-submission, absence, or failure to meet notified requirements
4.5 Issues arising from missed deadlines where timelines were duly notified
4.6 Disciplinary matters, including academic misconduct or unfair means, governed under the UPES Academic Integrity & Student Academic Misconduct Policy

## 5. EXAMINATION RE-EVALUATION & POST RE-EVALUATION GRIEVANCE

The examination re-evaluation and post re-evaluation grievance process is governed by examination-specific mechanisms prescribed under University regulations and operates independently of the School-level academic appeal process outlined in this Policy.

## 1.1 Eligibility for Re-evaluation

5.1.1 A student may apply for re-evaluation of an End-Term examination answer script after declaration of results, in accordance with University rules.
5.1.2 Re-evaluation is available only for End-Term examinations and only through the process defined in this Policy.

## 5.2 Authority

5.2.1 All matters relating to examination re-evaluation and post re-evaluation grievance redressal are governed by the Controller of Examinations (CoE).
5.2.2 Schools, faculty members, Programme Leads, and Deans do not handle examination re-evaluation or post re-evaluation grievances.

## 5.3 Mode of Application

5.3.1 Applications for re-evaluation shall be submitted only through the University ERP system, within the timeline notified after result declaration.
5.3.2 Applications submitted through email, physical representation, or informal communication shall not be accepted.
5.3.3 The prescribed re-evaluation fee shall be paid online at the time of application.

## 5.4 Re-evaluation Process

1.a.1 Upon receipt of a valid re-evaluation application, the Controller of Examinations shall arrange for re-evaluation of the answer script in accordance with approved University procedures.
1.a.2 Re-evaluation is conducted independently of the original evaluation.
1.a.3 After completion of re-evaluation, the revised result, if any, shall be processed by the CoE.
1.a.4 The student shall be informed only of the revised grade or result, and not the numerical marks or detailed evaluation.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 5.5 Outcome of Re-evaluation

5.5.1 The outcome of re-evaluation may result in **no change, increase, or decrease** in the grade.

5.5.2 The result declared after re-evaluation shall replace the earlier result for all academic purposes.

1.1.3 If the student is satisfied with the outcome, the matter stands closed.

## 1.2 Grievance After Re-evaluation

5.6.1 If a student remains dissatisfied after completion of the re-evaluation, the student may submit a **grievance related to the re-evaluation outcome**.

1.a.2 This grievance does **not** constitute a second re-evaluation and does **not** involve fresh assessment of the answer script.

## 5.7 Grievance Redressal by University-Level Committee

5.7.1 A grievance raised after re-evaluation shall be placed before a **University-level grievance redressal committee** constituted for examination matters, as prescribed under University examination regulations.

5.7.2 The role of the committee is limited to examining the grievance related to the re-evaluation outcome in accordance with University examination procedures.

5.7.3 The committee shall **not** conduct any fresh evaluation, re-evaluation, or reassessment of the answer script.

5.7.4 The committee’s examination shall focus on whether the re-evaluation process was duly carried out as prescribed.

## 5.8 Decision and Finality

5.8.1 The decision taken after grievance redressal by the University-level committee shall be **final and binding**.

5.8.2 The student shall be informed of the final outcome as per University procedure.

5.8.3 **No further appeal, representation, or petition** shall be entertained after this stage in relation to the same examination.

## 5.9 Important Clarifications for Students

5.9.1 Examination re-evaluation and post re-evaluation grievance processes are **separate from School-level academic appeals**.

5.9.2 Faculty members, Programme Leads, Deans, and Schools **cannot intervene** in examination re-evaluation or grievance decisions.

5.9.3 Requests for disclosure of answer scripts, evaluator identity, or detailed marking are **not permitted**.

## 6. ACADEMIC APPEALS (NON-EXAMINATION)

Academic appeals under this Policy relate to **non-examination academic decisions** and are limited strictly to **procedural errors, administrative or clerical errors, data or record-related errors, or incorrect application of published academic rules**.

Academic appeals **shall not** involve re-evaluation of academic merit, quality of answers, grading discretion, or policy relaxation.

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

The categories of academic appeals covered under this section are detailed below.

### 6.1 Continuous Assessment (CA) Appeals

6.1.1 Continuous Assessment constitutes 70% of course evaluation (Internal Assessment + Mid-Course Evaluation),
6.1.2 Appeals shall not be entertained on academic judgment, marks awarded, grading stringency, or comparative performance.

**Permissible CA appeals are limited to:**

6.1.3 Incorrect tabulation or upload of CA marks
6.1.4 Non-adherence to notified rubrics or approved assessment schemes
6.1.5 Demonstrable procedural irregularity or bias
6.1.6 Verified LMS or system-related errors

### 6.2 Attendance-Related Appeals (Data & Computation Only)

Attendance-related appeals may be raised only for data correction or computation errors.

**Permissible attendance appeals include:**

6.2.1 Errors in attendance data entry
6.2.2 Errors in attendance percentage computation
6.2.3 Verified ERP or system-related discrepancies affecting attendance records

**The following shall not be appealable:**

6.2.4 Attendance condensation requests
6.2.5 Policy-based attendance relaxations
6.2.6 Appeals based on illness, personal circumstances, academic performance, or lack of awareness

### 6.3 Prerequisite Enforcement Appeals

Appeals relating to prerequisite enforcement may be considered only on procedural or system-related grounds.

**Permissible prerequisite appeals include:**

6.3.1 Incorrect prerequisite mapping
6.3.2 ERP or system error resulting in incorrect prerequisite blocking

**The following shall not be appealable:**

6.3.3 Waiver of prerequisites due to poor academic performance or failure
6.3.4 Graduation pressure, placement timelines, or personal inconvenience
6.3.5 Appeals violating UGC / AICTE / University regulations

### 6.4 Registration & Academic Progression Appeals

Appeals relating to registration or academic progression may be considered only where an error in application of rules or records is established.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

Permissible appeals include:
- 6.4.1 Registration block due to data or system error
- 6.4.2 Incorrect application of progression or eligibility rules

The following shall not be appealable:
- 6.4.3 Requests for relaxation of minimum credit or progression requirements
- 6.4.4 Appeals seeking exceptions to maximum Programme duration norms

6.5 Credit Mapping, Equivalence & Transfer Appeals
Appeals relating to credits may be considered only on record or mapping errors.

Permissible credit appeals include:
- 6.5.1 Credit equivalence applied incorrectly
- 6.5.2 Approved credits not reflected in the academic record

The following shall not be appealable:
- 6.5.3 Discretionary credit approval requests
- 6.5.4 Appeals challenging statutory or curriculum-approved credit frameworks

6.6 Academic Mobility Appeals
Academic mobility appeals include decisions relating to:
- 6.6.1 Change of Programme
- 6.6.2 Change of Major
- 6.6.3 Change of Specialisation
- 6.6.4 Change of Minor

Such appeals shall be considered only on procedural grounds.

Permissible academic mobility appeals include:
- 6.6.5 Error in processing the application
- 6.6.6 Data or record-related error
- 6.6.7 Incorrect application of published eligibility conditions

The following shall not be appealable:
- 6.6.8 Academic judgment regarding suitability or feasibility
- 6.6.9 Capacity constraints or cut-offs
- 6.6.10 Missed timelines duly notified
- 6.6.11 Discretionary decisions taken in accordance with approved policy

Academic mobility appeals shall be submitted in writing to the Pro-Vice Chancellor / Vice Provost for consideration.

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

### 6.7 Incorrect Application of Published Academic Rules

Appeals may be raised where a student is adversely affected due to incorrect interpretation or application of a published academic policy, regulation, or rule.

Such appeals shall:

6.7.1 Be examined strictly on documentary evidence
6.7.2 Not result in creation of new academic entitlements or policy relaxations

### 6.8 General Conditions Applicable to All Academic Appeals

6.8.1 Appeals must be submitted within the officially notified timelines, where applicable.
6.8.2 Appeals shall not entitle students to provisional or conditional registration pending final decision.
6.8.3 Submission of false, misleading, or fabricated information shall result in summary rejection and may attract disciplinary action.

## 7. SCHOOL-LEVEL ACADEMIC APPEAL PROCESS

All non-examination academic appeals shall follow the mandatory sequential escalation below.

|  Appeal Level | Authority | When Applicable | Role & Responsibilities | Outcome / Status  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  Level 1 | Concerned Faculty Member | Initial stage of all non-examination academic appeals | • Student approaches the concerned faculty member directly
• Attempts resolution of the academic issue at the instructional level | Matter resolved or escalated to next level  |
|  Level 2 | Programme Lead | If the issue remains unresolved at the Faculty level | • Reviews procedural compliance
• Examines relevant academic records and documentation
• Issues a reasoned response | Matter resolved or escalated to next level  |
|  Level 3 (Final) | Dean of the School | If the issue remains unresolved at the Programme Lead level | • Reviews procedural correctness only
• Bases decision strictly on documented evidence
• Issues a reasoned and written decision | Final and binding decision
No further appeal permitted  |

## 8. MODE OF SUBMISSION & RECORD MANAGEMENT

8.1 Academic appeals under Section 6 shall be submitted to School Office using (Form Annexure - 1)

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

8.2 Records shall be maintained by the School
8.3 Academic mobility appeals to be submitted to PVC/Vice Provost by the student.

## 9. EVIDENCE REQUIREMENTS

9.1 Appeals must be supported by relevant, verifiable documentary evidence.
9.2 Unverifiable, altered, misleading, or fabricated evidence shall result in summary rejection.
9.3 Submission of false evidence may attract action under the Student Disciplinary Code.

## 10. CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-RETALIATION

10.1 All academic appeal and re-evaluation proceedings shall be handled confidentially.
10.2 Access to records shall be restricted to authorised personnel only.
10.3 UPES strictly prohibits retaliation against students who raise academic appeals or re-evaluation requests in good faith.

## 11. FINALITY & CLOSURE

Once the prescribed appeal or re-evaluation pathway under this Policy has been exhausted:

11.1 No further appeal, representation, or correspondence shall be entertained on the same matter.
11.2 This includes representations addressed to the Vice Chancellor outside the scope permitted under this Policy.

## 12. EXCLUSION OF ACADEMIC MISCONDUCT

Academic misconduct, unfair means, plagiarism, or integrity violations shall be governed exclusively by the UPES Academic Integrity & Student Academic Misconduct Policy and shall not be routed through this Policy.

## 13. POLICY REVIEW

This Policy shall be reviewed every three (3) years or earlier if required by regulatory changes or institutional requirements.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
0078.summary: UPES policy provides students structured framework to appeal non-exam academic decisions and request exam re-evaluation for procedural/administrative errors or rule misapplications.

0079.title: ACADEMIC INTEGRITY & STUDENT ACADEMIC MISCONDUCT POLICY
0079.text:
# ACADEMIC INTEGRITY & STUDENT ACADEMIC MISCONDUCT POLICY

(Including Unfair Means during Examinations)

(Policy Code – AG 17)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 1. Purpose

1.1 The Academic Integrity & Student Academic Misconduct Policy establishes a comprehensive, transparent, and institutionally consistent framework to:

1.1.1 Define Academic Integrity and Academic Misconduct, including Unfair Means (UFM) during examinations

1.1.2 Prevent, detect, investigate, and adjudicate academic misconduct

1.1.3 Ensure due process, proportionality, and adherence to principles of natural justice

1.1.4 Protect the credibility and integrity of UPES academic qualifications

1.1.5 Safeguard student rights while maintaining rigorous academic standards

1.2 This Policy is corrective and educative in intent, while remaining firm against conduct that compromises academic integrity.

1.3 This Policy shall operate in alignment with:

1.3.1 The Academic Recovery Pathways (ARP)

1.3.2 The Academic Grievance & Appeals Policy

1.3.3 The Student Disciplinary Policy

1.3.4 Examination and Allied Provisions (as notified by the Controller of Examinations)

## 2. Scope

2.1 This Policy applies to:

2.1.1 All Undergraduate (UG), Postgraduate (PG), Doctoral, and Certificate students of UPES

2.1.2 All Schools and academic units

2.1.3 All academic assessments and submissions, including:

- Continuous Assessment (CA)
- Mid-Course Evaluation (MCE)
- End-Term Examinations
- Practical, laboratory, studio, viva assessments
- Projects, dissertations, theses
- Online, hybrid, remote-proctored, or take-home examinations

2.2 This Policy applies irrespective of the mode of delivery or assessment.

## 3. What This Policy Governs

3.1 This Policy governs Academic Misconduct, defined as acts that compromise the integrity, fairness, or credibility of academic assessment, evaluation, or research.

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

### 3.2 What This Policy Does NOT Govern

This Policy does not govern:

- Academic performance disputes without misconduct
- Dissatisfaction with marks based solely on academic judgment
- Attendance condensation decisions
- Academic progression, probation, recovery, or entitlement decisions

Such matters shall be governed by the relevant UPES Academic Policies.

## 4. Definitions

|  Term | Definition  |
| --- | --- |
|  Academic Integrity | Adherence to ethical principles of honesty, trust, fairness, respect, and responsibility in learning, teaching, assessment, and research.  |
|  Academic Misconduct | Any act or attempted act by a student that provides an unfair academic advantage or compromises the integrity of the academic process.  |
|  Unfair Means (UFM) | Any act, attempt, or assistance during an examination that violates examination rules or compromises fairness or security.  |
|  Fit-to-Sit Principle | A student appearing for an assessment is deemed fit to sit, unless exceptional circumstances prevented prior disclosure.  |
|  Academic Judgment | Professional evaluation of academic work by faculty based on approved rubrics and learning outcomes. Academic judgment is not appealable.  |

## 5. Categories of Academic Misconduct

|  Category | Nature & Description | Illustrative Examples  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  Examination-Related Misconduct | Dishonest conduct connected to assessments compromising fairness | Copying, answer sharing, unauthorized materials  |
|  Plagiarism | Presenting another's intellectual work as one's own | Copy-paste without citation, self-plagiarism  |
|  AI-Related Misconduct | Misuse of AI tools that misrepresents authorship | Submitting AI-generated work without disclosure  |
|  Fabrication & Falsification | Manipulation or creation of false academic data | Invented data, altered records  |
|  Misrepresentation | Providing false information for academic benefit | Forged medical certificates  |

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

|  Research Misconduct | Ethical violations in research | Data suppression, unethical experimentation  |
| --- | --- | --- |

## 6. Unfair Means (UFM) During Examinations

### 6.1 Definition

Unfair Means (UFM) refers to any act, attempt, or assistance during examinations that compromises integrity.

Attempted UFM shall be treated at par with committed UFM.

### 6.2 Detection & Reporting

UFM may be detected and reported by:

- Invigilators / Proctors
- Examination Supervisors
- Controller of Examinations (CoE)
- Online Proctoring Systems
- Faculty (based on post-examination evidence)

All cases shall be:

- Documented using prescribed format
- Supported by material evidence
- Forwarded to the CoE

### 6.3 Classification of UFM

|  UFM Level | Nature | Illustrative Examples | Indicative Penalty | Governance Notes  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  Level U1 - Minor | Procedural / inadvertent | Possession without use | Cancellation of component + warning | Repetition escalates  |
|  Level U2 - Major | Clear intent or usage | Copying, device use | F grade in course; no supplementary | Overrides recovery entitlements  |
|  Level U3 - Severe | Organized or egregious | Impersonation, tampering | Semester cancellation / suspension | May trigger disciplinary proceedings  |

### 6.4 Adjudication Mechanism

6.4.1 All UFM cases shall be adjudicated by the University Academic Integrity Committee (UAIC)

6.4.2 School-level committees shall not adjudicate examination UFM

6.4.3 Penalties shall be implemented by the CoE after approval

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

### 6.5 Interface with Recovery Policies

6.5.1 UFM penalties determine the academic outcome for the course
6.5.2 Where UFM penalty specifies ineligibility for Supplementary / Summer / DR, such restriction shall prevail
6.5.3 Recovery pathways cannot override confirmed UFM penalties

### 6.6 Appeals in UFM Cases

Appeals may be filed only on procedural grounds:

- Violation of due process
- Incorrect identification
- Evidence handling errors

Appeals shall be governed by the Academic Grievance & Appeals Policy.

Academic judgment regarding evidence shall not be re-examined.

## 7. Levels of Non-Examination Academic Misconduct

### 7.1 Level A – Minor Academic Misconduct

|  Aspect | Provision  |
| --- | --- |
|  Nature | Inadvertent, low-impact violation  |
|  Examples | Improper citation, low similarity plagiarism  |
|  Penalties | Warning, deduction of marks, workshop, resubmission  |
|  Record Status | Internal record only  |

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

### 7.2 Level B - Moderate Academic Misconduct

|  Aspect | Provision  |
| --- | --- |
|  Nature | Clear violation with inferred intent  |
|  Examples | Substantial plagiarism, undeclared AI use  |
|  Penalties | F grade in component/course  |
|  Recovery | Governed by ARP subject to penalty  |
|  Escalation | Repeat offence → Level C  |

### 7.3 Level C - Severe Academic Misconduct

|  Aspect | Provision  |
| --- | --- |
|  Nature | Serious, deliberate misconduct  |
|  Examples | Fabrication of research data  |
|  Penalties | F grade + suspension  |
|  Record Status | Formal academic record  |

### 7.4 Level D - Gross / Egregious Misconduct

|  Aspect | Provision  |
| --- | --- |
|  Nature | Fraudulent or malicious conduct  |
|  Examples | Credential fabrication, organized cheating  |
|  Penalties | Termination / Degree revocation (where permissible)  |
|  Record Status | Permanent institutional record  |

## Governance Notes

- Academic judgment is not appealable
- Penalties are proportionate and progressive
- Repeat offences escalate automatically
- Recovery pathways cannot override confirmed penalties

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 8. Reporting & Preliminary Review (Non-UFM)

8.1 Misconduct may be reported by faculty or academic offices
8.2 Prima facie review shall be conducted
8.3 Level A cases may be resolved at School level unless contested

## 9. Investigation & Hearing

|  Level | Adjudicating Authority  |
| --- | --- |
|  Level A | Faculty Member  |
|  Level B | School Academic Integrity Committee (SAIC)  |
|  Level C & D | University Academic Integrity Committee (UAIC)  |

Principles:

- Right to be informed
- Right to be heard
- Conflict-of-interest disclosure
- Documented proceedings

## 10. Decision & Communication

10.1 Decisions shall be reasoned and documented
10.2 Communicated via ERP
10.3 Reflected in academic records where applicable

## 11. Appeals

11.1 Appeals permitted only on:

- Procedural irregularity
- Bias
- Disproportionate penalty

11.2 Appeals shall not re-assess academic judgment
11.3 Governed by Academic Grievance & Appeals Policy

## 12. Interface with Student Disciplinary Policy

12.1 Academic and disciplinary proceedings may run in parallel
12.2 Sanctions shall be coordinated to avoid duplication

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

13. Record Keeping & Confidentiality
13.1 Records retained for minimum 5 years
13.2 Access restricted to authorized personnel
13.3 Confidentiality strictly maintained

14. Non-Retaliation
UPES strictly prohibits retaliation against any student reporting or contesting misconduct in good faith.

15. Policy Review
This Policy shall be reviewed every three (3) years or earlier if required by regulatory changes or institutional requirements.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
0079.summary: This policy defines academic misconduct, establishes procedures to prevent/detect/adjudicate violations, and applies to all UPES students across all programs.

0080.title: Student Discipline Policy/Code of Conduct/Proctorial Board (Disciplinary Committee Constitution/Functions/Process of Handling Complaint)
0080.text:
# Student Discipline Policy/Code of Conduct/Proctorial Board (Disciplinary Committee Constitution/Functions/Process of Handling Complaint)

(Policy Code: DS-01)

This Policy shall come into force with immediate effect.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 1. Purpose

1.1 UPES is committed to maintaining a safe, inclusive, disciplined, and academically enriching environment for all members of the University community. Discipline is integral to character building, academic excellence, mutual respect, and responsible citizenship.

1.2 This Student Discipline Policy and Code of Conduct is framed in consonance with the UPES Regulations Governing Maintenance of Discipline among Students, 2009 (as amended from time to time) and applicable statutory provisions.

## 2. Applicability

This Policy shall apply to:

2.1 All students of UPES, irrespective of Programme, school, year of study, or mode of enrolment.

2.2 All activities conducted within or outside the campus, including hostels, internships, industrial visits, fieldwork, online platforms, and off-campus events.

2.3 All students admitted before or after the notification of this Policy.

## 3. Objectives of the Policy

The objectives of this Policy are to:

3.1 Promote discipline, integrity, and ethical conduct among students

3.2 Ensure a safe, respectful, and inclusive academic environment

3.3 Prevent acts of indiscipline, misconduct, harassment, ragging, and violence

3.4 Define expected standards of student behaviour and accountability

3.5 Provide a transparent framework for handling disciplinary violations

## 4. Code of Conduct for Students

All students of UPES shall:

4.1 Maintain good conduct, orderly behaviour, and discipline within and outside the campus

4.2 Uphold the values, traditions, and reputation of the University

4.3 Devote themselves sincerely to academic pursuits and maintain a conducive learning environment

4.4 Treat faculty, staff, fellow students, and visitors with respect, dignity, and courtesy

4.5 Comply with all University rules, regulations, policies, and lawful instructions issued from time to time

4.6 Refrain from any act that may bring disrepute to the University

January 2026

UPES
UNIVERSITY OF TOMORROW

## 5. Types of Misconduct

Any act of misconduct committed inside or outside the campus and formally reported shall be construed as a violation of discipline. Acts of indiscipline can be categorized into following categories:

5.1 Classroom Misconduct
5.2 Minor Act of Indiscipline/ General Misconduct
5.3 Major Act of Indiscipline
5.4 Zero Tolerance offences
5.5 Off Campus Misconduct

## Failure to Appear Before Authorities

If a student fails to appear before authorities when required for any of the above categories of indiscipline, Chief proctor/Proctorial Board can take following action:

- Suspension from attending classes until compliance
- Blocking of attendance portal
- Intimation to parents/guardians
- Suspension revoked only after reporting to the concerned authority

## 6. Disciplinary Probation

The Chief Proctor may place a student on disciplinary probation:

6.1 Up to six (06) months on recommendation of the Proctorial Board Disciplinary Committee, or
6.2 For a period exceeding six (06) months, with approval of the Vice Chancellor

Disciplinary probation is a severe formal reprimand imposed after the establishment of misconduct.

## 7. Implications of Disciplinary Probation

7.1 Disciplinary probation signifies that the student's conduct is under strict institutional observation.
7.2 It covers misconduct occurring both within and outside the campus, including socially unacceptable behaviour that adversely affects the University's image.
7.3 Illustrative (not exhaustive) examples include:

7.3.1 Consumption of alcohol in public places
7.3.2 Public display of affection on campus or public spaces
7.3.3 Creating noise or disturbance in classrooms or corridors
7.3.4 Use of abusive or derogatory language
7.3.5 Derogatory remarks against UPES or its authorities on social media
7.3.6 Carrying food or beverages into classrooms or corridors
7.3.7 Unauthorized use of restricted washrooms
7.3.8 Misconduct during Industrial visits/ Educational tours/ Internships/Sports, cultural, co-curricular or extracurricular activities

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY

## 8. Conditions During Probation Period

During the probation period:

8.1 The student's conduct shall be closely monitored by the Proctor and Course Coordinator
8.2 Parents/guardians shall be informed and may be required to visit the University
8.3 The student shall be allowed to attend classes and marked "Present"
8.4 The student shall not participate in any co-curricular, extra-curricular, cultural event, or industrial visit without prior written permission of the School Discipline Controller

## 9. Appeal & Review

Students have the right to submit a written appeal for review of the decision to the Final Appellate Committee (FAC) within 10 days of the punishment order issued by the Chief Proctor Office, via email at fac@ddn.upes.ac.in.

January 2026

UPES UNIVERSITY OF TECHNOLOGY
